back_translation: 'If the thief is caught while breaking in and is struck so that he dies, there is no bloodguilt for him.'
'If the sun has risen upon him, there is bloodguilt for him. He must surely make restitution; if he has nothing, he shall be sold for his theft.'
'If the stolen item is actually found in his possession alive — whether ox, donkey, or sheep — he shall pay double.'
'If a man grazes a field or vineyard, and lets his livestock loose so that it grazes in another's field, from the best of his own field and the best of his vineyard he shall make restitution.'
'If a fire breaks out and finds thornbushes, and stacks of grain or standing grain or the field is consumed, the one who started the fire shall surely make restitution.'
'If a man gives his neighbor money or goods for safekeeping, and they are stolen from the man's house, if the thief is found, he shall pay double.'
'If the thief is not found, the owner of the house shall come near to the judges, to determine whether he has put his hand to his neighbor's property.'

'In every case of trespass — over ox, donkey, sheep, garment, or any lost thing about which one says, This is it — the case of both shall come before the judges; whomever the judges condemn shall pay double to his neighbor.'
'If a man gives his neighbor a donkey, ox, sheep, or any animal for safekeeping, and it dies, is injured, or is taken away while no one is watching,'
'an oath of the LORD shall be between the two of them, whether he has not put his hand to his neighbor's property; the owner shall accept it, and he shall not make restitution.'
'But if it was actually stolen from him, he shall make restitution to its owner.'
'If it was torn to pieces, he shall bring it as evidence; he shall not pay for what was torn.'
'If a man borrows from his neighbor, and it is injured or dies while its owner is not with him, he shall surely make restitution.'
'If its owner was with it, he shall not make restitution; if it was hired, the loss is covered by the hire fee.'
'If a man seduces a virgin who is not betrothed and lies with her, he shall surely pay the bride-price for her to be his wife.'
'If her father absolutely refuses to give her to him, he shall weigh out money according to the bride-price of virgins.'
'You shall not let a sorceress live.'
'Whoever lies with an animal shall surely be put to death.'
'Whoever sacrifices to any god shall be devoted to destruction, except to the LORD alone.'
'You shall not wrong a foreigner or oppress him, for you were foreigners in the land of Egypt.'
'You shall not afflict any widow or orphan.'
'If you do afflict them, and they cry out to me, I will surely hear their cry.'
'My anger will burn, and I will kill you with the sword; your wives shall become widows and your sons orphans.'
'If you lend money to my people, to the poor with you, you shall not be like a creditor to him; you shall not put interest on him.'
'If you take in pledge your neighbor's cloak, by sunset you shall return it to him.'
'For it is his only covering, his garment for his skin. In what shall he sleep? When he cries out to me, I will hear, for I am compassionate.'
'You shall not revile God, nor curse a ruler of your people.'
'You shall not delay your fullness and your juice. The firstborn of your sons you shall give to me.'
'You shall do likewise with your ox and your sheep. Seven days it shall be with its mother; on the eighth day you shall give it to me.'
'You shall be holy men to me. You shall not eat flesh torn in the field; to the dog you shall throw it.'
